```python
def _parse_nodes(tokens, idx, stop_tags):
    nodes = []
    while idx < len(tokens):
        tok_type, content = tokens[idx]
        if tok_type == 'text':
            nodes.append(TextNode(content))
            idx += 1
        elif tok_type == 'var':
            expr = content.strip()
            nodes.append(VariableNode(expr))
            idx += 1
        elif tok_type == 'tag':
            command = content.strip()
            # split first word
            parts = command.split(None, 1)
            tag_word = parts[0] if parts else ''
            # Check if stop tag
            if tag_word in stop_tags:
                return nodes, idx  # stop before consuming this tag
            # dispatch based on tag_word
            if tag_word == 'if':
                condition_expr = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                # parse body until elif, else, endif
                body_nodes, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('elif', 'else', 'endif'))
                # now token at idx is one of stop_tags; check which
                # build branches list: (condition_expr, body_nodes)
                branches = [(condition_expr, body_nodes)]
                # handle elif/else
                while idx < len(tokens):
                    tok2 = tokens[idx]
                    if tok2[0] != 'tag':
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError
                    cmd2 = tok2[1].strip()
                    parts2 = cmd2.split(None, 1)
                    tw2 = parts2[0] if parts2 else ''
                    if tw2 == 'elif':
                        elif_cond = parts2[1].strip() if len(parts2) > 1 else ''
                        body2, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('elif', 'else', 'endif'))
                        branches.append((elif_cond, body2))
                    elif tw2 == 'else':
                        # else body, stop_tags=('endif',)
                        body2, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endif',))
                        branches.append((None, body2))  # None condition for else
                    elif tw2 == 'endif':
                        # consume endif
                        idx += 1
                        break
                    else:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError
                else:
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError("Unexpected end of template looking for endif")
                nodes.append(IfNode(branches))
            elif tag_word == 'for':
                # parse "var in expr"
                # content after 'for ': e.g., "item in items"
                rest = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                match = re.match(r'(\w+)\s+in\s+(.+)', rest, re.DOTALL)
                if not match:
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError
                loop_var = match.group(1)
                seq_expr = match.group(2).strip()
                body, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('else', 'endfor'))
                # check stop tag
                if idx < len(tokens):
                    tok2 = tokens[idx]
                    cmd2 = tok2[1].strip()
                    tw2 = cmd2.split()[0] if cmd2.split() else ''
                    else_body = None
                    if tw2 == 'else':
                        else_body, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endfor',))
                        # now expect endfor
                        if idx >= len(tokens) or tokens[idx][0] != 'tag' or tokens[idx][1].strip().split()[0] != 'endfor':
                            raise TemplateSyntaxError
                        idx += 1  # consume endfor
                    elif tw2 == 'endfor':
                        idx += 1  # consume endfor
                    else:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError
                else:
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError
                nodes.append(ForNode(loop_var, seq_expr, body, else_body))
            elif tag_word == 'block':
                # block name
                name = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                body, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endblock',))
                if idx >= len(tokens) or tokens[idx][0] != 'tag' or tokens[idx][1].strip().split()[0] != 'endblock':
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError
                idx += 1  # consume endblock
                nodes.append(BlockNode(name, body))
            elif tag_word == 'with':
                # with name = expr
                rest = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                # split by '='
                if '=' not in rest:
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError
                var_part, expr_part = rest.split('=', 1)
                var_name = var_part.strip()
                # validate var_name is identifier?
                if not var_name.isidentifier():
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError
                value_expr = expr_part.strip()
                body, idx = _parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endwith',))
                if idx >= len(tokens) or tokens[idx][0] != 'tag' or tokens[idx][1].strip().split()[0] != 'endwith':
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError
                idx += 1  # consume endwith
                nodes.append(WithNode(var_name, value_expr, body))
            else:
                raise TemplateSyntaxError(f"Unknown tag: {tag_word}")
        else:
            # should not happen
            raise TemplateSyntaxError
    return nodes, idx
```

```
import re

class TemplateSyntaxError(Exception):
    pass

# Undefined sentinel
class _Undefined:
    _instance = None
    def __new__(cls):
        if cls._instance is None:
            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
        return cls._instance
    def __bool__(self):
        return False
    def __str__(self):
        return ''
    def __repr__(self):
        return 'Undefined'

UNDEFINED = _Undefined()

# AST nodes for template
class TextNode:
    def __init__(self, text):
        self.text = text
    def render(self, context, output):
        output.append(self.text)

class VariableNode:
    def __init__(self, expr_ast):
        self.expr = expr_ast
    def render(self, context, output):
        val = self.expr.evaluate(context)
        if not isinstance(val, _Undefined):
            output.append(str(val))

class IfNode:
    def __init__(self, branches):
        # branches: list of (condition_ast_or_None, body_nodes)
        self.branches = branches
    def render(self, context, output):
        for cond, body in self.branches:
            if cond is None or truth(cond.evaluate(context)):
                for node in body:
                    node.render(context, output)
                break

class ForNode:
    def __init__(self, loop_var, seq_expr_ast, body, else_body):
        self.loop_var = loop_var
        self.seq_expr = seq_expr_ast
        self.body = body
        self.else_body = else_body
    def render(self, context, output):
        seq_val = self.seq_expr.evaluate(context)
        if isinstance(seq_val, _Undefined):
            iterator = iter([])
        else:
            try:
                iterator = iter(seq_val)
            except TypeError:
                iterator = iter([])
        has_items = False
        for item in iterator:
            has_items = True
            context.append({self.loop_var: item})
            for node in self.body:
                node.render(context, output)
            context.pop()
        if not has_items and self.else_body:
            for node in self.else_body:
                node.render(context, output)

class BlockNode:
    def __init__(self, name, body):
        self.name = name
        self.body = body
    def render(self, context, output):
        for node in self.body:
            node.render(context, output)

class WithNode:
    def __init__(self, var_name, value_expr_ast, body):
        self.var_name = var_name
        self.value_expr = value_expr_ast
        self.body = body
    def render(self, context, output):
        val = self.value_expr.evaluate(context)
        context.append({self.var_name: val})
        for node in self.body:
            node.render(context, output)
        context.pop()

def truth(val):
    if isinstance(val, _Undefined):
        return False
    return bool(val)

# Expression AST nodes
class Literal:
    def __init__(self, value):
        self.value = value
    def evaluate(self, context):
        return self.value

class Name:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
    def evaluate(self, context):
        for scope in reversed(context):
            if self.name in scope:
                return scope[self.name]
        return UNDEFINED

class Getattr:
    def __init__(self, base, attr):
        self.base = base
        self.attr = attr
    def evaluate(self, context):
        base_val = self.base.evaluate(context)
        if isinstance(base_val, _Undefined):
            return UNDEFINED
        try:
            return getattr(base_val, self.attr)
        except AttributeError:
            try:
                return base_val[self.attr]
            except (KeyError, IndexError, TypeError):
                return UNDEFINED

class Compare:
    def __init__(self, left, op, right):
        self.left = left
        self.op = op
        self.right = right
    def evaluate(self, context):
        left_val = self.left.evaluate(context)
        right_val = self.right.evaluate(context)
        if isinstance(left_val, _Undefined) or isinstance(right_val, _Undefined):
            return False
        try:
            if self.op == '==':
                return left_val == right_val
            elif self.op == '!=':
                return left_val != right_val
            elif self.op == '<':
                return left_val < right_val
            elif self.op == '>':
                return left_val > right_val
            elif self.op == '<=':
                return left_val <= right_val
            elif self.op == '>=':
                return left_val >= right_val
            elif self.op == 'in':
                return left_val in right_val
            elif self.op == 'not in':
                return left_val not in right_val
            else:
                return False
        except TypeError:
            return False

class NotExpr:
    def __init__(self, operand):
        self.operand = operand
    def evaluate(self, context):
        val = self.operand.evaluate(context)
        return not truth(val)

class Group:
    def __init__(self, expr):
        self.expr = expr
    def evaluate(self, context):
        return self.expr.evaluate(context)

class ExpressionParser:
    def __init__(self, tokens):
        self.tokens = tokens
        self.pos = 0
    
    def peek(self):
        if self.pos < len(self.tokens):
            return self.tokens[self.pos]
        return ('EOF', None)
    
    def advance(self):
        tok = self.peek()
        self.pos += 1
        return tok
    
    def expect(self, typ, value=None):
        tok = self.advance()
        if tok[0] != typ:
            raise TemplateSyntaxError(f"Expected {typ}, got {tok[0]}")
        if value is not None and tok[1] != value:
            raise TemplateSyntaxError(f"Expected {value}")
        return tok
    
    def parse(self):
        return self.comparison()
    
    def comparison(self):
        left = self.unary()
        tok = self.peek()
        if tok[0] == 'OP' or tok[0] == 'IN' or tok[0] == 'NOT_IN':
            op_tok = self.advance()
            op = op_tok[1]
            right = self.unary()
            # We'll not allow chaining; whatever after is ok? We'll just return Compare.
            return Compare(left, op, right)
        return left
    
    def unary(self):
        tok = self.peek()
        if tok[0] == 'NOT':
            self.advance()
            operand = self.unary()
            return NotExpr(operand)
        return self.primary()
    
    def primary(self):
        tok = self.peek()
        if tok[0] == 'NAME':
            name = tok[1]
            self.advance()
            expr = Name(name)
            while self.peek()[0] == 'DOT':
                self.advance()  # consume dot
                attr_tok = self.expect('NAME')  # attribute must be NAME
                expr = Getattr(expr, attr_tok[1])
            return expr
        elif tok[0] in ('STRING', 'NUMBER', 'TRUE', 'FALSE', 'NONE'):
            val = tok[1]
            self.advance()
            return Literal(val)
        elif tok[0] == 'LPAREN':
            self.advance()
            expr = self.parse()
            self.expect('RPAREN')
            return Group(expr)
        else:
            raise TemplateSyntaxError(f"Unexpected token {tok}")
```

class Template:
    def __init__(self, source):
        self.nodes = self._parse(source)
    
    def _parse(self, source):
        tokens = self._tokenize_template(source)
        nodes, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, 0, ())
        if idx != len(tokens):
            # leftover tokens should not happen
            raise TemplateSyntaxError("Unexpected tokens")
        return nodes
    
    def _tokenize_template(self, source):
        # use regex to split
        tag_re = re.compile(r'\{\{(.+?)\}\}|{%(.+?)%}', re.DOTALL)
        tokens = []
        last_end = 0
        for m in tag_re.finditer(source):
            start = m.start()
            if start > last_end:
                text = source[last_end:start]
                tokens.append(('text', text))
            if m.group(1) is not None:
                tokens.append(('var', m.group(1).strip()))
            else:
                tokens.append(('tag', m.group(2).strip()))
            last_end = m.end()
        if last_end < len(source):
            tokens.append(('text', source[last_end:]))
        return tokens
    
    def _parse_nodes(self, tokens, start_idx, stop_tags):
        idx = start_idx
        nodes = []
        while idx < len(tokens):
            tok_type, content = tokens[idx]
            if tok_type == 'text':
                nodes.append(TextNode(content))
                idx += 1
            elif tok_type == 'var':
                expr_ast = parse_expr(content)
                nodes.append(VariableNode(expr_ast))
                idx += 1
            elif tok_type == 'tag':
                command = content.strip()
                # get first word
                parts = command.split(None, 1)
                tag_word = parts[0] if parts else ''
                if tag_word in stop_tags:
                    return nodes, idx  # stop and don't consume
                # dispatch
                if tag_word == 'if':
                    condition_text = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                    if not condition_text:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("if tag requires condition")
                    cond_ast = parse_expr(condition_text)
                    body, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('elif', 'else', 'endif'))
                    branches = [(cond_ast, body)]
                    while idx < len(tokens):
                        tok2 = tokens[idx]
                        if tok2[0] != 'tag':
                            raise TemplateSyntaxError("Expected elif/else/endif")
                        cmd2 = tok2[1].strip()
                        parts2 = cmd2.split(None, 1)
                        tw2 = parts2[0] if parts2 else ''
                        if tw2 == 'elif':
                            elif_cond_text = parts2[1].strip() if len(parts2) > 1 else ''
                            if not elif_cond_text:
                                raise TemplateSyntaxError("elif tag requires condition")
                            elif_cond_ast = parse_expr(elif_cond_text)
                            body2, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('elif', 'else', 'endif'))
                            branches.append((elif_cond_ast, body2))
                        elif tw2 == 'else':
                            body2, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endif',))
                            branches.append((None, body2))
                        elif tw2 == 'endif':
                            idx += 1
                            break
                        else:
                            raise TemplateSyntaxError
                    else:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("Unclosed if block, expected endif")
                    nodes.append(IfNode(branches))
                elif tag_word == 'for':
                    rest = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                    match = re.match(r'(\w+)\s+in\s+(.+)', rest, re.DOTALL)
                    if not match:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("Invalid for syntax")
                    loop_var = match.group(1)
                    seq_expr_text = match.group(2).strip()
                    seq_ast = parse_expr(seq_expr_text)
                    body, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('else', 'endfor'))
                    else_body = None
                    if idx < len(tokens):
                        tok2 = tokens[idx]
                        if tok2[0] != 'tag':
                            raise TemplateSyntaxError
                        cmd2 = tok2[1].strip()
                        tw2 = cmd2.split()[0] if cmd2.split() else ''
                        if tw2 == 'else':
                            else_body, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endfor',))
                            if idx >= len(tokens) or tokens[idx][0] != 'tag' or tokens[idx][1].strip().split()[0] != 'endfor':
                                raise TemplateSyntaxError("Expected endfor after else")
                            idx += 1
                        elif tw2 == 'endfor':
                            idx += 1
                        else:
                            raise TemplateSyntaxError
                    else:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("Unclosed for block")
                    nodes.append(ForNode(loop_var, seq_ast, body, else_body))
                elif tag_word == 'block':
                    block_name = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                    if not block_name:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("Block tag requires name")
                    body, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endblock',))
                    if idx >= len(tokens) or tokens[idx][0] != 'tag' or tokens[idx][1].strip().split()[0] != 'endblock':
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError
                    idx += 1
                    nodes.append(BlockNode(block_name, body))
                elif tag_word == 'with':
                    rest = parts[1].strip() if len(parts) > 1 else ''
                    if '=' not in rest:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("with tag requires assignment")
                    var_part, expr_part = rest.split('=', 1)
                    var_name = var_part.strip()
                    if not var_name.isidentifier():
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("Invalid variable name in with")
                    value_expr_text = expr_part.strip()
                    if not value_expr_text:
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("with tag requires value")
                    value_ast = parse_expr(value_expr_text)
                    body, idx = self._parse_nodes(tokens, idx+1, stop_tags=('endwith',))
                    if idx >= len(tokens) or tokens[idx][0] != 'tag' or tokens[idx][1].strip().split()[0] != 'endwith':
                        raise TemplateSyntaxError("Expected endwith")
                    idx += 1
                    nodes.append(WithNode(var_name, value_ast, body))
                else:
                    raise TemplateSyntaxError(f"Unknown tag: {tag_word}")
        return nodes, idx
